jokes.text: What's the sauce with the fish? / There was no fish. / Was fish the second thing?
jokes.setup: Waiter describing menu items in Italian, customers confused
jokes.punchline: They're debating about fish that was never mentioned
jokes.explanation: Classic Curb setup - miscommunication escalates into absurd debate about nonexistent fish

jokes.text: I'll just have the first thing. / Okay, and I'll have the second thing.
jokes.setup: They don't understand the Italian menu
jokes.punchline: They order by position rather than understanding what they're getting
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical way to show their confusion - they've given up on understanding

jokes.text: They should write the thing down. I don't know what he's talking about. I've no idea what we just ordered.
jokes.setup: Larry's frustration with the Italian restaurant experience
jokes.punchline: His blunt assessment of the communication failure
jokes.explanation: Pure Larry David - practical complaint about restaurant pretension

jokes.text: Is it ever closed? Has it ever been closed to anything?
jokes.setup: Cheryl asks Larry to keep an open mind
jokes.punchline: Larry's sarcastic response about his allegedly always-open mind
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Larry's self-aware sarcasm about his own closed-mindedness

jokes.text: You got it. / Are you serious? / Nothing big, small.
jokes.setup: Cheryl expects resistance to renewing vows
jokes.punchline: Larry immediately agrees, subverting her expectation
jokes.explanation: Misdirection works because audience expects Larry to resist

jokes.text: What do you want to be a David for? Davids want to be out of being Davids. I don't want to be a David, I have to be a David. But if you don't have to be a David, why be a David?
jokes.setup: Cheryl wants to take Larry's last name
jokes.punchline: Larry's circular logic about why being a David is undesirable
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-deprecating logic spiral that's both absurd and relatable

jokes.text: How about if we get married now... like, right now... for our 10-year anniversary, you can have sex with someone else?
jokes.setup: Larry seems reluctant about marriage
jokes.punchline: Cheryl offers him a hall pass to seal the deal
jokes.explanation: Shocking offer that drives the episode's main plot - unexpected and character-revealing

jokes.text: What?! If that's your hold up here, then let's just do that.
jokes.setup: Cheryl's casual offer of infidelity permission
jokes.punchline: Larry's shocked reaction followed by Cheryl doubling down
jokes.explanation: Larry's genuine shock sells the absurdity of Cheryl's proposal

jokes.text: Oh, right! / Right. / I can't believe you remember that.
jokes.setup: They're talking about some past agreement
jokes.punchline: Larry suddenly remembers the 10-year-old conversation
jokes.explanation: Callback reveals that this was actually discussed before, making it more absurd

jokes.text: Hey, lady. I'm Larry David And my wife said I could have sex with somebody tonight.
jokes.setup: Larry imagining how he'd approach someone for the hall pass
jokes.punchline: His awkward, overly honest pickup line
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Larry's complete social ineptitude - the worst possible pickup line

jokes.text: Even if I wanted to, you don't think I'm capable of it. / No, I don't think you're capable of it. I really don't.
jokes.setup: Larry realizes Cheryl doesn't think he can seduce anyone
jokes.punchline: Cheryl's brutally honest confirmation
jokes.explanation: Devastating marital honesty that's both funny and uncomfortable

jokes.text: Listen, why don't we go home and I'll... I'll make it worth your while. / I kind of wanna go to karaoke.
jokes.setup: Cheryl offers sexual incentive to skip karaoke
jokes.punchline: Larry still prefers karaoke
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection showing Larry's social priorities over sexual ones

jokes.text: This is the thing that I'm the worst at in the world, this. This and drawing.
jokes.setup: Larry discussing his inability to pick up women
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to his drawing skills as his two worst abilities
jokes.explanation: Specific, unexpected comparison that reveals Larry's self-awareness

jokes.text: It's like a third thing to do after bowling and the movies. I don't know if you bowl. I don't go that often, But it's fun, it's fun. You can't find a ball, that's the problem.
jokes.setup: Larry awkwardly trying to make conversation about karaoke
jokes.punchline: Immediately pivots to bowling ball complaints
jokes.explanation: Captures Larry's terrible conversation skills and tendency to over-explain

jokes.text: My whole life, every time I'm in a bowling alley Sticking my fingers in all these holes, picking up the balls. You gotta get your own ball. I don't bowl enough to get my own ball. It takes up a lot of space in the house. You keep looking at it in the closet going, 'What I am doing with a bowling ball? I don't even bowl.'
jokes.setup: Continuing the bowling tangent while trying to flirt
jokes.punchline: Detailed analysis of bowling ball ownership economics
jokes.explanation: Larry's social awkwardness shown through over-detailed bowling analysis

jokes.text: How do you get rid of a bowling ball? Think about that. Who do you give a bowling ball to? Nobody bowls. The thing, it only fits your fingers. You throw a bowling ball in the garbage can, you know what that sanitation man's gonna do? He's gonna knock on your door, that's how upset he's gonna be. He's gonna say, 'Who the fuck threw a bowling ball in the garbage?'
jokes.setup: Problem of bowling ball disposal
jokes.punchline: Imagining the angry garbage man's reaction
jokes.explanation: Escalates to absurd but logical conclusion with angry garbage man scenario

jokes.text: Okay, all right.
jokes.setup: Woman has endured Larry's entire bowling monologue
jokes.punchline: Her polite but clear dismissal
jokes.explanation: Perfect beat showing the failure of Larry's approach

jokes.text: What the hell are you doing? / What the fuck are you doing? What am I doing? What are you doing? What the fuck? I'm trying to walk. / While you're talking on a cell phone? You almost killed me, douche bag!
jokes.setup: Near collision in parking lot
jokes.punchline: Immediate escalation to mutual insults
jokes.explanation: Typical Larry conflict escalation with added uncomfortable disability element

jokes.text: Yeah, well, he's in a... he's in a wheelchair. / Yeah, I know. It's a moving vehicle. There's got to be some kind of regulations I would think, even for people in electric chairs. He's motoring along. You don't need to be talking on a cell phone. It's dangerous.
jokes.setup: Larry complaining about wheelchair user's driving
jokes.punchline: His insistence on treating wheelchair like a vehicle requiring regulations
jokes.explanation: Larry's oblivious application of logic to sensitive situation

jokes.text: You got yourself a partner. I got a wife. Not exactly a partner. More like... a rival. You know what I mean? It's a rivalry. I wish I could say this is my partner.
jokes.setup: Receptionist mentions her partner
jokes.punchline: Larry's cynical view of marriage as rivalry
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest observation about marriage dynamics

jokes.text: Well you got, uh... you got Wang from the Good Earth. Remember Wang Lung? Wang isn't a bad first name. Wang? Then you got the whole Ang family. Fang, Bang... Tang.
jokes.setup: Discussing Chinese baby names
jokes.punchline: Larry's culturally insensitive rhyming name suggestions
jokes.explanation: Relies on cheap cultural humor but fits Larry's character obliviousness

jokes.text: Tang is not actually a bad name, 'cause it's like China but it's... it's not China. It's a juice is what it is. Is that so bad naming a kid after a juice? Maybe that's not so bad. But it has Chinese overtones... Tang.
jokes.setup: Larry justifying Tang as a name
jokes.punchline: His circular logic about juice/China connection
jokes.explanation: Larry's overthinking creates absurd justification for bad suggestion

jokes.text: Uh, that's gonna be the least of his problems, no?
jokes.setup: Receptionist worries about schoolkid teasing over name Tang
jokes.punchline: Larry implies bigger problems with being adopted Chinese kid
jokes.explanation: Dark but economical observation about adoption/racial challenges

jokes.text: Oh my God, what... Larry! Larry David. Oh my God! Ooh, did I hurt you?
jokes.setup: Larry going to bathroom, meets his comedy idol
jokes.punchline: Gets injured by Mel Brooks with a bathroom door
jokes.explanation: Perfect irony - meeting your comedy hero who immediately injures you. Sets up the main Broadway subplot nicely.

jokes.text: Then sign Ben Vereen. Who cares what you sign? Come on. Your head's bleeding. Just sign in!
jokes.setup: Larry refusing to put real name on doctor's sign-in
jokes.punchline: Jeff's exasperated suggestion to sign fake name
jokes.explanation: Good escalation beat showing Jeff's frustration with Larry's neuroses

jokes.text: Sorry. It was a local call. / You're not supposed to use the phone. It doesn't matter whether it was a local or a long distance call. We just don't want people using that phone, okay?
jokes.setup: Larry used doctor's phone, thinks local call justifies it
jokes.punchline: Doctor's firm rejection of the local call defense
jokes.explanation: Sets up Larry's inability to accept arbitrary rules

jokes.text: I don't understand why not, though. Why couldn't I use it? / Well, you could use it. You obviously did. We don't want you to use it, though.
jokes.setup: Larry demanding logical explanation for phone rule
jokes.punchline: Doctor's circular non-explanation
jokes.explanation: Classic Curb interaction of Larry vs arbitrary authority

jokes.text: The history of how we arrived at that Is so complex that I won't even bore you with it. Just let it be said that we don't allow anyone To use the phones in the examination rooms.
jokes.setup: Larry wants explanation for phone rule
jokes.punchline: Doctor claims complex history but won't explain
jokes.explanation: Absurd bureaucratic response that frustrates Larry further

jokes.text: Nothing more, I would say, than a little prick. / Yeah, there's definitely a prick involved. / Uh, yes, there is a prick involved. / Yeah, there's one prick involved. / Yeah, I'd say there is one prick involved. / I agree. I'm not a doctor, but I agree there is one prick.
jokes.setup: Doctor describes stitches as 'little prick'
jokes.punchline: Extended wordplay about there being 'one prick' (the doctor)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that lets Larry insult the doctor indirectly

jokes.text: You drooled on me!
jokes.setup: Doctor working on Larry's stitches
jokes.punchline: Doctor accidentally drools on patient
jokes.explanation: Gross-out gag that adds to Larry's medical humiliation

jokes.text: My big fat friend. Hmm, fatso... Yeah, fatso.
jokes.setup: Larry affectionately calling Jeff fat
jokes.punchline: Escalating from 'big fat friend' to 'fatso'
jokes.explanation: Running gag about Jeff's weight, mild callback to earlier comment

jokes.text: She said she found the wallet, gave it to me and I gave her $100. / Seriously, you should worry a little bit less about the wallet And maybe maintain a little focus on your driving skills, buddy.
jokes.setup: Dennis reveals receptionist lied about finding wallet
jokes.punchline: Dennis deflects by attacking Larry's driving instead
jokes.explanation: Nice deflection that callbacks to their first encounter

jokes.text: You think it's good to name a kid after a breakfast drink?! We don't think that's a good name! Do you understand that? / You want to name our baby Tang?!
jokes.setup: Larry's earlier Tang suggestion comes back
jokes.punchline: Partner's outrage at the breakfast drink name suggestion
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows consequences of Larry's casual insensitivity

jokes.text: And when you sang, I was absolutely floored. I mean, l... you think l... Really? No no, I couldn't get over it. And bang bang, something went off in me.
jokes.setup: Mel praising Larry's karaoke performance
jokes.punchline: Larry's disbelief and Mel's enthusiasm about his 'discovery'
jokes.explanation: Sets up the main plot twist with Larry's incredulous reaction

jokes.text: I want you, Larry David, to be the next Max Bialystock on Broadway. I would like you to play the lead in 'The Producers.'
jokes.setup: Building up to a big reveal
jokes.punchline: Offering Larry David a Broadway lead role
jokes.explanation: Major plot revelation that's both absurd and believable given the setup

jokes.text: 'Or you got it or you ain't.' I said, 'Andreas, you can't start a sentence with 'or.'' He said, 'Never mind. Or you got it, or you ain't.'
jokes.setup: Mel telling story about Andreas the Greek actor
jokes.punchline: Grammar correction followed by stubborn repetition
jokes.explanation: Classic Mel Brooks storytelling with character voices and grammar humor

jokes.text: Giant fuckin' mistake if I say so right to your face. Okay, Zero Mostel... Yeah, I know. I know what you're gonna say. Nathan Lane, Jason Alexander and Larry David? Come on, Mel. / Trust me. When I know, I know. And this time, I know.
jokes.setup: Jeff listing the prestigious actors who played the role
jokes.punchline: The absurdity of Larry David following that lineage
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment highlighting how unlikely Larry's casting is

jokes.text: Mel Brooks hit me with a bathroom door. Cut my head. I know, it sounds like a joke.
jokes.setup: Larry explaining his injury to Ben Stiller
jokes.punchline: Acknowledging how absurd it sounds
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor acknowledging the absurdity of the situation

jokes.text: You're fucking with me, right? / No, it's true, but I'm still thinking about it. I haven't made up my mind.
jokes.setup: Ben can't believe Larry was offered the role
jokes.punchline: Larry's casual attitude about the Broadway opportunity
jokes.explanation: Larry's nonchalance about major opportunity is perfectly in character

jokes.text: Why didn't you shake his hand? He put his hand out for you. / He sneezed all over his hand. But he just sneezed on it. / How rude is that? / He didn't notice anything.
jokes.setup: Larry refused to shake Ben Stiller's hand after he sneezed
jokes.punchline: Larry's logical hygiene concern vs social rudeness
jokes.explanation: Classic Larry prioritizing hygiene logic over social convention

jokes.text: You know, maybe I will. Maybe I will.
jokes.setup: Larry watching The Producers performance
jokes.punchline: His growing confidence about taking the role
jokes.explanation: Closes episode on Larry's growing acceptance of the absurd opportunity
